expectations.evidence: 3 distinct emails: Welcome & Quick Win, Feature Spotlight (Timelines), Full Picture (Automated Reports).

expectations.text: Each email has a subject line
expectations.evidence: Each email has a subject line. No preview text provided (unlike with_skill output).

expectations.text: Copy emphasizes benefits over raw features
expectations.evidence: Does translate features to benefits reasonably well: 'clear picture of what everyone is working on — without pulling anyone into a call', 'See milestones, blockers, and progress at the project level.'

expectations.text: Each email has a clear, specific CTA (not generic 'learn more')
expectations.evidence: CTAs are present and somewhat specific: 'Set Up Your First Standup', 'Build Your First Timeline', 'Turn On Automated Reports'. Email 3 has a secondary CTA 'Book an Onboarding Call' which slightly dilutes focus.

expectations.text: Tone is conversational and professional, not stiff corporate
expectations.evidence: Conversational with contractions and direct address. Slightly more instructional/tutorial-like than peer-to-peer, but not stiff.

expectations.text: Emails follow logical progression (welcome → value → conversion)
expectations.evidence: Follows immediate → Day 2 → Day 5 progression. Moves from setup to feature spotlight to full value pitch + onboarding call offer.

expectations.text: Provides multiple variations or strategic approaches
expectations.evidence: Only one version of the sequence provided. No variations, no alternative approaches, no A/B testing suggestions.
